prompt:
The ESLint 9 conversion is breaking our monorepo lint job. The first fatal says `project tsconfig.json does not own packages/api/src/handler.ts`; attempts to move on from that also start linting generated route/schema output. We have separate API and web TypeScript projects, and tests intentionally use a small amount of `any` and console output under their existing override. Protected local notes and a hermetic evaluator capture the flat-config rules without an npm install.

Finish the flat configuration so every source and test file is analyzed with its owning package project, generated and distribution trees are ignored globally, and the test-only exceptions apply after the type-aware package rules. Keep the strict type-checked rule set and CI's zero-warning behavior: warnings must still fail even when there are no errors. Do not disable type-aware linting, broaden test exceptions into production, or edit source fixtures to make the configuration appear clean.

Run `node --test test_eslint_flat_config.ts`. The test, evaluator, CI helper, rule set, package tsconfigs, source fixtures, and migration notes are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: translate a multi-package ESLint cascade into ordered flat configuration with explicit project ownership, global ignores, overrides, and CI severity semantics
full_distill.baseline_evidence: node --test test_eslint_flat_config.ts exits 1 because the baseline flat entry assigns ./tsconfig.json to package sources and the protected evaluator reports that project does not own packages/api/src/handler.ts
full_distill.success_contract: API and web source/tests lint through their owning tsconfigs, generated and dist paths are globally absent, documented test overrides remain narrow, strict type-aware rules remain active, and any warning still makes CI exit 1
adversarial_factors: a counterexample config applies the API tsconfig to web files and must produce a project-boundary diagnostic, rejecting one root or cross-package project shortcut
generated fixtures contain explicit any, floating promises, and console calls while a protected local-ignore counterexample still exposes them, proving ignores must be global objects
removing the later test override produces both explicit-any errors and console warnings while project ownership remains active, rejecting disabling type-aware linting for all tests
an injected clean-project file produces exactly one warning and zero errors yet CI must exit 1, protecting maxWarnings zero from severity downgrades or warning suppression
